_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize GA state-legislature batch: mike-cameron@GA (HD-1 R), derek-mallow@GA (SD-2 D), steve-tarvin@GA (HD-2 R), mike-hodges@GA (SD-3 R), mitchell-horner@GA (HD-3 R). Evidence-first: every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence; SB140/SB233/HB1105 party-line votes were NOT scored where the member's individual roll-call could not be confirmed by name (LegiScan/legis.ga.gov blocked automated fetch). Horner SB233-2023 NO vote left NULL as genuinely ambiguous (conflicts with his stated pro-voucher position; 2024 re-vote unconfirmed).

profile.background: Republican, GA House District 1 (Dade + western Walker, NW Georgia); in office since Jan 2021, 3rd term (won 2024, 78.5%). 30+ yrs health-insurance industry.

mike-cameron@GA.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: consistent conservative record where documented; no contrary evidence found. HARD votes: SB233 school choice (YES), HB1358 constitutional carry (co-sponsor). iVoterGuide candidate survey + Georgia Life Alliance 2022 pro-life certification supply self-stated positions on life, marriage, religious liberty, voter ID, border. Many individual roll-calls (SB140, HB1105, SB63, SB202) not indexed by name -> left null. One ambiguous iVoterGuide answer on sanctuary-city funding noted but not scored (likely survey-wording artifact).

0.note: iVoterGuide survey: 'Human life begins at conception and deserves legal protection' (Strongly Agree); Georgia Life Alliance certified pro-life 2022.

1.note: iVoterGuide: 'I really don't see circumstances where abortion should be allowed' (no-exceptions self-stated position).

2.note: No public position found on embryonic stem-cell / IVF embryo discard / chimeric research.

3.note: No public position found on euthanasia / assisted suicide.

4.note: No cited donor record located; no evidence either way on abortion-industry PAC funding.

2.note: iVoterGuide: opposes adding gender identity as a protected class.

3.note: No position found on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: No specific evidence on LGBTQ promotion in schools/military/corporate-gov.

profile.background: Democrat, GA Senate District 2 (Savannah/Chatham); 2nd Senate term (sworn Jan 2025), Chief Deputy Whip; prior GA House HD-163 2021-2023.

derek-mallow@GA.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: documented progressive record, evidence-cited (NOT party-inferred). Authored SB15 (2023) to delete fetal personhood from GA's 'natural person' definition and SR136 (2023) constitutional amendment protecting abortion to viability; signed 2021 pro-choice SCOTUS amicus. Voted NO / argued against SB233 school-choice vouchers; opposed SB63 cash-bail (offered amendment to reduce restricted offenses); voted NO on HB1018 (2A protection); Freedom Index records Yes on HB872 (loan forgiveness) + HB206. No pro-life, pro-police, pro-gun, or pro-school-choice deviation found despite targeted search. SB140/SB202/SB319 (House-era/party-line) not scored where his name was not confirmed.

0.note: Sponsored SB15 (2023) to strip 'unborn child' from GA's natural-person definition (delete fetal personhood).

1.note: Sponsored SR136 (2023) constitutional amendment to protect abortion up to fetal viability; signed pro-choice SCOTUS amicus.

2.note: No specific position on embryonic stem-cell / IVF / chimeric research.

profile.background: Republican, GA House District 2 (Walker/Catoosa/Whitfield, NW Georgia); continuous service since Feb 2014 (no gap), reelected 2024. Conservative ratings: ACU 80%, Institute for Legislative Analysis 89% (2024)/75% lifetime.

steve-tarvin@GA.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: thick conservative record, no contrary evidence found. HARD: signed Georgia Life Alliance 2026 pro-life certification (incl. HB481/personhood YES, embryo-protection YES); Students for Life Action 100%; voted FOR SB140 (minor gender-care ban), SB233 (school choice ESA), HB1207 (election-worker citizenship), HB1105 (sanctuary-city ban). Campaign documents LEO pay raises. Euthanasia GLA Q25 marked 'unsure' -> null (not scored against). Roll-calls on SB319/SB63/SB202 not located by name -> null.

profile.background: Republican, GA Senate District 3 (coastal -- Brunswick/Glynn, Camden, Charlton, Brantley, McIntosh); in office since Jan 2023, reelected 2024. Banking background (Ameris Bancorp); Chair, Reapportionment & Redistricting.

mike-hodges@GA.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: genuinely thin record (in office since 2023; LegiScan/legis.ga.gov blocked automated fetch, so SB140/SB233/HB1105/SB63 party-line votes were NOT scored where his name could not be confirmed). Scored only HARD self-statements + sponsorships: anti-defund-police pledge; championed HB535 (mandatory fentanyl-trafficking penalties); sponsored SB370 (human-trafficking) and SB420 (foreign-farmland ban / Act 496). No contrary evidence and no issue-group endorsement (NRA/GRTL/F&F) found. Pre-2023 marquee bills (HB481, SB202, SB319) predate his term.

profile.background: Republican, GA House District 3 (most of Catoosa County, NW Georgia); first elected 2022, reelected 2024. Endorsed by Georgia Right to Life and Georgia Republican Assembly; Freedom Index 81%.

mitchell-horner@GA.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: conservative voting record (Freedom Index 81%), evidence-cited. HARD votes: NO on HB428 (IVF-access codification), YES on SB140 (minor gender-care ban), YES on SB36 (RFRA), YES on HB1053 (CBDC ban), YES on HR780 (citizen-only voting), YES on SB420 (foreign-land restriction). GRTL/iVoterGuide surveys supply personhood + photo-ID + border positions. DEVIATION FLAG: voted NO on SB233 (2023) Georgia Promise Scholarship -- conflicts with his stated 'strongly supports school choice incl. vouchers'; 2024 re-vote unconfirmed -> left NULL as genuinely ambiguous rather than scored as contrary.

0.note: GRTL candidate interview: supports establishing personhood; Georgia Right to Life endorsed.

1.note: GRTL interview: abortion allowed only for the life of the mother; opposes all government funding of abortion providers.

2.note: Voted NO on HB428 ('Right to In Vitro Fertilization' codifying IVF access) -> opposes IVF-access codification.

4.note: GRTL-endorsed and opposes PP funding, but no cited donor record; none located.

0.note: Did not answer on religious marriage definition.

1.note: No cited vote.

2.note: Voted YES on SB140 (minor gender-care ban); iVoterGuide: strongly opposes adding gender identity as a protected class.

0.note: AMBIGUOUS: voted NO on SB233 (2023, failed 85-89) -- contrary -- yet iVoterGuide says he 'strongly supports school choice incl. vouchers'; 2024 re-vote unconfirmed. Left null rather than mis-score.
